parsed_review.summary: CP00-357 is a descriptor-only RP11 "failure binding slice" pack (RP11.P07.M04.S07-RP11.P07.M04.S16, 10 units, risk A). Read-only inspection confirms all baseline parameters match across the live closeout-pack-plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:413, non-circular), manifest, contract current_pack + cp357_requirements + embedded failure_binding_slice_descriptor projection, registry/service/validators source, validator script, and tests: range, unit_count=10, deliverable distribution (failure_recovery:6/security_audit:1/implementation:1/hermes_evidence:1/fixture:1), and micro_title (Secondary Workflow Slice:10) are all consistent. The descriptor is purely data construction (Object.freeze, no I/O/network/DB); freezeCp357Result and validateTimeExpenseCoreCp357FailureBindingSliceDescriptor enforce descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe/no-raw-payload on every row plus the required no-leak guards (cross-tenant access denied, permission-decision/blocked-claim/lock-token/fixture-payload/hermes-packet-body detail excluded, unauthorized-data omitted). Per-row guard fields are correctly inherited from the CP355 row-extras chain, and the contract deepEqual against the generated descriptor holds. production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; the production_ready_flag is descriptor-verification-scoped only, so there is no enterprise-trust over-claim. Authority boundary holds (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false); Hermes is used only as a deterministic evidence-packet descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening preflight is clean: auth passed with 1456-byte valid JSON (not 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped), allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob only, schema present, no forbidden review evidence, and no invalid attempts attributed to this pack (the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt file is correctly scoped out). Downstream artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent as expected pre-review. Only a single P3 cosmetic nit found (dead row-extras key). No P0/P1/P2 issues; safe to proceed to human-gated closeout.

findings.title: Dead `documentation_entry` key in CP357 row-extras map (no-op, propagates via spread)
findings.details: TIME_EXPENSE_CORE_CP357_ROW_EXTRAS adds a top-level `documentation_entry: { documentation_entry: "packages/time-expense/README.md#cp00-357" }` key. Row extras are applied via `...(TIME_EXPENSE_CORE_CP357_ROW_EXTRAS[key] ?? {})` where key = timeExpenseCoreRowKey(title), and none of the 10 CP00-357 failure row titles (Cross-tenant failure ... Failure fixture) resolve to the key `documentation_entry`, so the entry is never applied to any row. The README anchor is already correctly set at the descriptor level (service.js:2278) and the README #cp00-357 section exists. The stray key is therefore a no-op that also propagates forward into future packs' row-extras via the spread chain. No functional, validation, or security impact: the slice validator passes, the contract failure_binding_slice_descriptor deepEqual matches, and tests assert valid=true.
findings.recommendation: Remove the unused `documentation_entry` entry from TIME_EXPENSE_CORE_CP357_ROW_EXTRAS (descriptor-level documentation_entry already covers it), or, if forward-seeding for a future row is intended, add a brief comment noting that intent so it is not mistaken for a row-key extras entry.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic cleanup. Does not affect the descriptor-verified production_ready claim, no-leak/no-write boundaries, authority boundary, or any closeout gate. CP00-357 is safe to close; optionally address in a later pass.
